meta.title: SF-SPADA-1: Advanced SQL, Cortex AI and Semi-Structured Data

questions.stem: You need the latest order per customer without nesting another query. Which Snowflake feature is MOST directly suited for filtering the output of a window function?
questions.explanation: QUALIFY filters rows after window functions are computed, making it ideal for patterns like ROW_NUMBER() = 1. HAVING applies to grouped results, not windowed row filtering.

questions.stem: A report needs subtotals by region, by product, and a grand total in one query. Which construct BEST supports this?
questions.explanation: GROUPING SETS family constructs produce multiple aggregation levels in one query. LIMIT, masking policies, and COPY INTO are unrelated.
questions.reference: Snowflake SQL GROUPING SETS, ROLLUP, and CUBE documentation

questions.stem: An analyst must compare each day's revenue with the previous day for the same store. Which function is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation: LAG accesses a prior row in a window partition, which is ideal for day-over-day comparisons. The other functions serve different purposes.

questions.stem: JSON event payloads are stored in a VARIANT column and contain an array of line items. Which function is used to turn each array element into a separate row?
questions.explanation: FLATTEN explodes array or object content from VARIANT data into rows that can be queried relationally.

questions.stem: Some source strings are invalid JSON and should not fail the whole query. Which function BEST parses valid JSON while returning NULL for invalid records?
questions.explanation: TRY_PARSE_JSON returns NULL on invalid input instead of raising an error, which is useful in messy analyst pipelines.

questions.stem: You are transforming nested JSON into analyst-friendly columns. Select TWO correct statements. (Select TWO)

options.text: Snowflake supports dot and bracket notation when querying fields inside VARIANT values
Analysts often need explicit casts when turning VARIANT fields into numeric or string report columns
VARIANT data cannot be joined with relational tables
JSON arrays can be expanded only after exporting to Python

questions.explanation: VARIANT fields can be accessed with JSON path notation and then cast into relational types for downstream analysis. Semi-structured data can still be joined and processed inside Snowflake.

questions.stem: An analyst wants to summarize large support notes directly in SQL without deploying a separate model. Which Snowflake capability BEST fits?

options.text: Snowflake Cortex AI functions such as SUMMARIZE
An external function to a BI tool

questions.explanation: Cortex AI functions provide managed AI capabilities directly from SQL for tasks like summarization and classification.

questions.stem: A team needs natural-language answers grounded in a governed semantic model for business users. Which feature is MOST aligned?
questions.explanation: Cortex Analyst is designed for natural-language analytics grounded in semantic models. The other features solve infrastructure or performance concerns.

questions.stem: What is the MOST important reason to think about cost when adding Cortex COMPLETE or SUMMARIZE to a dashboard refresh workflow?

options.text: LLM-backed functions can add measurable per-query compute or service cost at scale
Cortex disables result caching for all SQL
Cortex requires warehouses to run at 4XL or larger
AI functions automatically create permanent tables

questions.explanation: Managed AI calls can become expensive when applied to large numbers of rows or frequent refreshes, so analysts must consider volume and cadence.
questions.reference: Snowflake Cortex pricing and usage guidance

questions.stem: A dashboard runs the exact same query repeatedly and no underlying data or session settings have changed. Which Snowflake feature is MOST likely to return the result quickly without re-executing the query plan?
questions.explanation: Snowflake result caching can reuse results for repeated identical queries when the relevant conditions are met.

questions.stem: A large fact table is often filtered by date range and region. Which design choice may improve scan efficiency for those predicates over time?

options.text: Define a clustering key aligned to common filter columns

questions.explanation: Clustering can improve pruning for recurrent selective predicates such as date and region filters. The other options do not address table pruning.

questions.stem: An analyst is inspecting Query Profile and sees major spill to local and remote storage during joins. What does this MOST likely indicate?

options.text: The workload needs better tuning, a different plan, or more warehouse resources because memory pressure is occurring
The query used too many CTE names

questions.explanation: Spill signals that intermediate data exceeded available memory, which often points to join skew, large shuffles, or undersized compute.

questions.stem: Which Snowflake feature hides sensitive column values based on policy while still allowing analysts to query the table?
questions.explanation: Dynamic data masking applies policy-driven redaction at query time so protected data can remain in place while exposure is controlled.

questions.stem: A governed self-service analytics program is being designed. Select TWO practices that BEST support it. (Select TWO)

options.text: Use roles and grants so analysts receive only the access they need
Apply tags or policies to identify and protect sensitive assets
Share admin credentials for easier dashboard creation
Grant every analyst OWNERSHIP on production schemas

questions.explanation: Least-privilege access and clear classification/policy controls are the core of governed self-service. Shared admin credentials and broad ownership are risky anti-patterns.
questions.reference: Snowflake access control and object tagging documentation

questions.stem: A business team wants lightweight dashboards directly inside Snowflake's web experience. Which option BEST fits?
questions.explanation: Snowsight provides native worksheets, charts, and dashboards in the Snowflake interface. The other features serve different purposes.

questions.stem: An analyst needs an interactive Python data app close to Snowflake data without moving datasets to another platform. Which feature is MOST appropriate?
questions.explanation: Streamlit in Snowflake supports governed interactive Python apps that run near Snowflake data.

questions.stem: A dashboard depends on an expensive aggregation that changes only a few times per day. Which design is MOST likely to improve user-facing latency?

options.text: Precompute the aggregate with a materialized view or scheduled refresh table
Store the chart as JSON in a VARIANT column
Replace the warehouse with a masking policy

questions.explanation: Precomputed aggregates reduce repeated expensive work for dashboards. UI changes and masking policies do not optimize the underlying query workload.
questions.reference: Snowflake materialized views and tasks documentation
